title: M0-editor: CodeMirror 6 plain-text editor in the webview
intent: The webview displays a CodeMirror 6 editor instance. The user can type characters and see them appear immediately in the editor. No IPC yet — this item proves CodeMirror 6 is mounted, receives keystrokes, and maintains a local buffer.
description: Mount CodeMirror 6 in the Tauri webview with a minimal plain-text configuration. No markdown mode, no syntax highlighting, no decorations — raw text input only. The editor must be focusable, must accept keyboard input, and must maintain its content in a local JS buffer. Wire up a basic TypeScript/JS module that imports CodeMirror 6 packages via npm and initializes an EditorView. IPC bridge integration comes in M0-open-save-loop — this item ends when the editor is visible and accepts input.
steps: Add CodeMirror 6 packages as npm dependencies: @codemirror/view, @codemirror/state
Create src/editor.ts (or .js) that imports EditorView and EditorState from @codemirror/view and @codemirror/state
Initialize an EditorView with a minimal setup (basicSetup from codemirror package, or manual extensions list)
Mount the EditorView into a DOM element on index.html load
Verify the editor is visible in the webview and accepts keyboard input
Export a getContent() function from editor.ts that returns the current editor string content
Wire the build system (Vite or equivalent) to bundle the TypeScript/JS for the Tauri webview

acceptanceCriteria.text: npm install and npm run build both exit 0
Built output includes an index.html entry point
The built JS bundle contains CodeMirror EditorView code
The built bundle does NOT contain @codemirror/lang-markdown — M0 is plain-text-only

title: Phase1-lang-markdown: CodeMirror 6 markdown language extension and syntax highlighting
intent: The editor understands markdown structure — headings, bold, italic, code, links each render with distinct visual treatment. A plain-text view with no markdown awareness is not acceptable after Phase 1.
description:
Add @codemirror/lang-markdown to the frontend and configure it as the editor language. Add a syntax highlight style that visually distinguishes the key markdown token types. This replaces the plain-text CodeMirror setup from M0 with a proper markdown-aware one. After this item, WYSIWYM decorations (FEAT-013) become possible because the syntax tree is available.

Scope boundary: this item is highlighting only. WYSIWYM hiding/showing of syntax characters comes in FEAT-013. Do not add WYSIWYM behavior here.
steps: Add @codemirror/lang-markdown and @codemirror/language to package.json, run npm install
Replace the current plain-text CodeMirror setup in src/editor.ts with markdown() language extension
Define a HighlightStyle that assigns distinct styles to: ATXHeading, StrongEmphasis, Emphasis, InlineCode, FencedCode, Link, URL
Apply the HighlightStyle via syntaxHighlighting() in the CodeMirror extensions array
Verify the existing IPC open/save loop from FEAT-005 still works with the new language extension

acceptanceCriteria.text: npm run build succeeds with @codemirror/lang-markdown installed
cargo test --workspace passes — Rust core unaffected

acceptanceCriteria: Bold (**text**) and italic (*text*) tokens have distinct visual styling from plain text
Inline code and fenced code blocks are visually distinguished from prose

title: Phase1-the-render: Source-to-rich toggle animation (The Render)
intent: Toggling between source and rich view transforms the document — syntax characters animate out, styled content materializes. This is the single most important demo moment in the product. A mode swap without transition is not acceptable.
description:
Implement a source/rich toggle that, when activated, animates markdown syntax characters fading out while rendered elements (styled headings, bold text, rendered links) fade in. Target: ~400ms, spring-eased CSS transitions at 60fps.

Rich view is still CodeMirror — we use Mark and Replace decorations to hide/replace syntax characters with styled equivalents. This is not a renderer switch (no separate preview pane). It is a decoration pass on top of the same editor.

Reduced Motion alternative: instant crossfade (no animation, just state swap).

Trigger: Cmd+Shift+R (macOS) / Ctrl+Shift+R (other), or a visible toggle button in the UI.

Dependency: FEAT-013 (WYSIWYM) must land first since The Render extends the same decoration architecture.
steps: Add a richMode boolean state and a Compartment in src/editor.ts

Create src/render.ts: a DecorationSet builder that in rich mode replaces heading markers with styled spans, collapses ** ** markers and applies font-weight: bold via widget decorations, renders links as styled text
Add CSS transitions in src/style.css for .cm-rich-* classes (opacity, transform, ~400ms spring)
Implement a toggleRender() command that flips richMode and reconfigures the compartment
Wire Cmd+Shift+R / Ctrl+Shift+R keymap to toggleRender()
Add a toolbar button that calls toggleRender() and shows current mode
Check prefers-reduced-motion: if set, apply changes without transition (instant swap)

acceptanceCriteria.text: npm run build succeeds with The Render animation included
